rows.title: Customers report checkout abandonment at shipping estimate
rows.body: Customer interviews from last sprint — 18 of 22 cited unexpected shipping cost as the main abandonment driver. Pattern repeats across US + EU cohorts. Page renders but estimate takes 1.4s p95.

rows.title: Search relevance regression after ranking model update
rows.body: Q2 ranking model swap caused 7% click-through drop on discovery. Engineering team noted that fallback path is not exercised; cold-start cases regress more than warm paths.

rows.title: Loyalty members asking for tier visibility in cart
rows.body: Three weeks of support tickets — Gold + Platinum members want their tier reflected at cart/checkout. Some churn because discount visibility is too subtle. Surfacing tier at cart time correlates with repeat purchase intent.

rows.title: GDPR consent banner removal roadmap
rows.body: EU customers report consent banner as friction. Region-aware routing eliminates the banner by routing EU traffic to EU pods only. Conversion lift of 18% on EU cohorts post-region-router rollout.

rows.title: Mobile wallet support — Apple Pay and Google Pay demand
rows.body: Mobile checkout funnel analysis — 38% of mobile users drop at the card-entry step. Wallet support removes that step entirely. Customer requests from product council + 12 support tickets in Q2.

rows.title: Cart write-path latency postmortem
rows.body: p95 add-to-cart latency 180ms baseline; 4x peak on Black Friday. Node.js implementation cannot deliver sub-50ms p95 at peak. Rewrite in Go is on the Q3 plan.

rows.title: PCI scope narrowing — Envoy filter rollout
rows.body: Reduce PCI-DSS audit scope from 12 services to 1 service via Envoy filter + iptables egress rules. Compliance audit cost reduction 80% projected.

rows.title: Argo Rollouts blue/green to last 3 services
rows.body: Catalog, search, and notifications still on rolling updates. Sub-second cutover needed for peak weeks. Argo Rollouts cluster is healthy and SRE is ready.

rows.title: [#acme-platform-eng] Customers report checkout abandonment at shipping estimate
rows.body: Anna: "Three more customers on support cited shipping cost as the abandonment driver. Same pattern as last week." Bob: "We have the data — page renders but estimate takes 1.4s p95. Recommendation: precompute estimates for top SKUs."

rows.title: [#acme-incidents] Search relevance regression after ranking model update
rows.body: Oncall: "Search CTR down 7% since the ranking model swap at 14:00 UTC. Rolling back is non-trivial; investigating warm vs cold paths."

rows.title: [#acme-deploys] Loyalty tier visibility request from product
rows.body: Maria: "Product council wants tier badge at cart time. Easy lift, big retention impact. Prioritizing for sprint 14."

rows.title: [#acme-platform-eng] EU GDPR banner removal plan
rows.body: Dan: "Region router is in canary; once we cut over, EU customers stop seeing the banner. Compliance team is aligned."

rows.title: [#acme-platform-eng] Mobile wallet integration kickoff
rows.body: Priya: "Wallet SDK integration kicks off this sprint. Apple Pay first, Google Pay next sprint. PRD already drafted."

rows.title: [#acme-platform-eng] Cart service Go migration kickoff
rows.body: Mike: "Cart team has Go expertise confirmed. Migration plan dual-write for 8 weeks, then cutover. RFC ready for review."

rows.title: [#acme-security] PCI scope reduction Envoy filter tested
rows.body: SecOps: "Envoy filter for PCI boundary in staging. Cluster-wide rollout next sprint. Audit team pre-aligned."

rows.title: [#acme-deploys] Argo Rollouts catalog migration complete
rows.body: SRE: "Catalog service now on blue/green. Two services left: search + notifications."

rows.title: Ticket: Shipping cost surprises on checkout
rows.body: Customer: "I added 3 items to cart and the shipping cost appeared as $24 at checkout. That's almost 40% of the order. I left and bought elsewhere." Priority: high. Status: open.

rows.title: Ticket: Search results not what I expected
rows.body: Customer: "Searched for 'wool coat' and got polyester blends first. The filter for material doesn't help." Priority: medium. Status: open.

rows.title: Ticket: Gold tier discount not visible
rows.body: Customer (Gold member): "I noticed my discount wasn't applied until the final review page. Made me almost abandon twice. Show me earlier?" Priority: high. Status: open.

rows.title: Ticket: Why am I seeing a GDPR cookie banner?
rows.body: Customer (EU): "I'm in Germany and the cookie banner pops up every page. Why can't I just have my preferences respected?" Priority: medium. Status: open.

rows.title: Ticket: Apple Pay option missing
rows.body: Customer (iOS user): "Why no Apple Pay? Every other store has it. It's 2026." Priority: medium. Status: open.

rows.title: Ticket: Add to cart slow today
rows.body: Customer: "Adding things to my cart is taking 2-3 seconds today. Usually it's instant. Something's off." Priority: high. Status: investigating.

rows.title: Ticket: Card declined 3 times then worked
rows.body: Customer: "Card kept getting declined — then it worked. Strange." Priority: low. Status: closed (intermittent).

rows.title: Ticket: Search returned 0 results
rows.body: Customer: "Searched for 'Nike Air Max' and got nothing. Tried 'nike air max' same result." Priority: medium. Status: closed (transient).

rows.title: Payment decline pattern after Stripe API shift
rows.body: Payments on-call noticed intermittent declines around 10:00 UTC. Pattern aligns with Stripe's regional latency event. Multi-vendor payments would have caught this.

rows.title: [#acme-incidents] Payment decline spike
rows.body: Oncall: "We're seeing intermittent Stripe declines. Customers blame the store. Two refunds requested."

rows.title: Ticket: Cart says item out of stock after adding
rows.body: Customer: "I added a jacket to my cart, then it disappeared. Refreshed and got 'out of stock'." Priority: medium. Status: investigating.

rows.title: Inventory staleness during high-traffic events
rows.body: Inventory cache TTL of 30s causes customers to add items that are actually out of stock. Cache invalidation is the bottleneck. Streaming invalidation reduces overcount by 90%.

rows.title: [#acme-platform-eng] Inventory staleness investigation
rows.body: SRE: "Cache TTL is the bottleneck. Testing streaming invalidation; early results show 90% reduction in stale cart issues."

rows.title: Ticket: Where's my order tracking?
rows.body: Customer: "I ordered 3 days ago and have no tracking link. Just an email saying it's shipping. Confused." Priority: medium. Status: open.

rows.title: Order tracking gap — 22% of weekly tickets
rows.body: Customer service tickets about order status account for 22% of weekly volume. Self-service tracking page would deflect 70%+ of these. Data is available; UI is the gap.

rows.title: [#acme-deploys] Order tracking page in design review
rows.body: Frontend: "Order tracking page is in design review. Two more iterations then ready for build."

rows.title: Ticket: Search ranking inconsistent across devices
rows.body: Customer: "I searched 'running shoes' on my phone, then on desktop — different top results." Priority: low. Status: investigating.

rows.title: Failed zendesk pull — 502 upstream
rows.body: Puller returned 502 from zendesk on 2026-07-04 22:00 UTC. 0 signals ingested that hour. Next run at 23:00 succeeded.
